operations.name: Bootstrap ReAct agent
operations.trigger: User wants a tool-calling agent with no custom routing, parallelism, or state beyond messages.
operations.action: Use `from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent` with a model and tools list. Add a checkpointer + thread_id for memory.
operations.output: Compiled graph supporting .invoke(), .stream(), with built-in message history and tool loop.

operations.name: Promote prebuilt agent to custom StateGraph
operations.trigger: Need state fields beyond messages, parallel branches, supervisor pattern, custom retry, or complex routing.
operations.action: Re-implement with `StateGraph(MyTypedDict)`; manually add nodes for LLM and tools; add `add_conditional_edges` on tool_calls; compile with checkpointer.
operations.output: Graph with explicit topology and full control.

operations.name: Add reducer to resolve InvalidUpdateError
operations.trigger: Two parallel nodes write the same state key; graph raises InvalidUpdateError.
operations.action: Change `key: list[X]` to `key: Annotated[list[X], operator.add]` (or `add_messages` for chat). For non-list types, write a custom reducer.
operations.output: Parallel writes merge correctly instead of conflicting.

operations.name: Dynamic fan-out with Send API
operations.trigger: At runtime, number of parallel tasks is determined by state (e.g., one summarizer per retrieved chunk).
operations.action: From a conditional edge, return `[Send('worker', {'chunk': c}) for c in state['chunks']]`. Worker uses its own state schema; results reduced into parent state.

operations.name: Insert human approval gate via interrupt()
operations.trigger: Node would perform an irreversible action (charge card, send email, run DELETE).
operations.action: Call `decision = interrupt({'proposed': payload})` BEFORE side effect, with side effect in a downstream node. Compile with persistent checkpointer. Caller resumes with `Command(resume=...)`.
operations.output: Thread pauses, state persists, resumes with human decision threaded back.

operations.name: Encapsulate subflow as a subgraph
operations.trigger: Logical chunk deserves isolation (research agent inside larger workflow, reusable validation pipeline).
operations.action: Compile child graph; add as node with `parent.add_node('research', subgraph)`. If schemas differ, wrap in a node function that translates.
operations.output: Reusable, independently testable module; child state isolated unless keys overlap.

operations.name: Migrate to Postgres checkpointer for production
operations.trigger: Moving from notebook to production OR multi-user OR thread state must survive deploys.
operations.action: Use `AsyncPostgresSaver`; pass to `.compile(checkpointer=...)`. Run `await saver.setup()` as a CI/CD migration, not at app boot. Implement TTL sweep for abandoned threads.
operations.output: Threads survive crashes; multi-replica safe; ACID guarantees.

operations.name: Stream tokens to UI while keeping graph observability
operations.trigger: Need char-by-char UX AND server-side debugging.
operations.action: Use `graph.stream(input, stream_mode=['messages', 'updates'])`. For intra-tool progress, emit via `stream_mode='custom'`.
operations.output: User sees streaming tokens; server logs state diffs.

operations.name: Bound an agent loop with explicit exit condition
operations.action: Add a retry counter to state (`retries: Annotated[int, operator.add]`). Increment on failure; conditional edge routes to END after N tries. Surface error content to LLM. Do NOT just raise `recursion_limit`.

operations.name: Time-travel debug a failed production run
operations.trigger: Production thread produced wrong answer; need to inspect state at step N and try alternatives.
operations.action: Fetch history via `graph.get_state_history(config)`; invoke with `config={'configurable': {'thread_id': ..., 'checkpoint_id': ...}}`. Modify with `graph.update_state(...)` to fork.
operations.output: Reproduce + fork past run without re-paying for replayed LLM calls.

operations.name: Choose supervisor vs swarm multi-agent topology
operations.trigger: Designing a multi-agent system; uncertain about pattern.
operations.action: Apply two binary tests: (1) Do sub-agents know each other? (2) Is a single user-facing voice mandatory? Both yes -> supervisor. Both no -> swarm. Mixed -> supervisor with LangChain's three documented fixes (remove handoff messages, forwarding-messages tool, tool naming optimization).
operations.output: Topology aligned with constraints; documented perf fixes applied.

operations.name: Idempotency belt for resumable nodes
operations.trigger: Node performs external side effect and is reachable from an interrupted resume path.
operations.action: Generate idempotency key in state BEFORE entering side-effect node. Pass key to external SDK so re-execution is a no-op. Move side effect to a downstream node from any interrupt-bearing node.
operations.output: Side effects safe under resume re-runs.

dilemma_cases.title: Supervisor vs swarm in multi-domain customer support
Side effects before interrupt() re-execute on resume

anti_patterns: AP-2: Using recursion_limit as control flow
AP-5: Sharing parallel-written keys without a reducer
AP-6: Running checkpointer.setup() at app boot
AP-7: Leaving interrupted threads to rot (no TTL)
AP-8: Using MessageGraph for new code
AP-10: Send-API fan-out for fixed cardinality
AP-11: Trusting LLM-only loops to terminate
AP-12: Bloating state with large blobs
AP-13: Relying on subgraph state visible during execution
AP-14: Conflating create_react_agent with full LangGraph

ecosystem_position.vs_crewai: LangGraph for production durability; CrewAI for hour-scale prototyping. Common pattern: prototype on CrewAI, port to LangGraph.
ecosystem_position.vs_autogen: AutoGen better for self-debugging code-gen; but in maintenance mode in 2026.
ecosystem_position.vs_swarm: Swarm is reference / educational only; not production.
ecosystem_position.vs_plain_langchain: LangChain for stateless flows / single LLM call; LangGraph for cycles, state, durability.

caveats: v1 Python docs were marked 'Alpha - content incomplete' as recently as early 2026.
Maintainer responsiveness on agent-loop bugs is uneven (issue #6731 closed 'not planned').
SQL injection CVE reported in SQLite checkpointer in March 2026; use Postgres for production.
